content:
Member-only story

Everyone shipped an agent this year. Almost nobody solved the boring part: the browser. I spent a day with BrowserAct, the CLI that just took #1 on Product Hunt, to see if the boring part is finally handled.

8 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Source — LinkIf you watch any agent demo, from the year you will see something interesting. The way it thinks is really impressive. The model makes a plan goes back and fixes mistakes that you might have missed. Then it opens a browser and the video stops. But what you do not see is what happens next: the Cloudflare warning page the CAPTCHA, the login screen the fingerprint check that catches the session in a very short time and the large amount of website code the model has to look through to find one button.
This missing part has a name now. Agents need a browser layer, not just a browser. A real Chrome browser is made for a person using a mouse. An agent needs something simple text pages, actions that can be done with commands, identities that stay separate for each task and a smooth way to let a human take over when a website needs one.
BrowserAct is trying to fill this gap. It was launched on Product Hunt in June and became the number one product of the day and number three…
